page: Microsoft Azure D96lds_v5 is a compute-optimized virtual machine belonging to the DLDSv5 family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 96 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 48 physical cores of an Intel Xeon 8370C processor operating at 2.8 GHz. The memory configuration consists of 192.0 GB of RAM, yielding a resource density of 2.0 GB per vCPU. This instance includes 3686 GB of local storage and does not feature any GPUs.

Benchmark data indicates strong multi-core performance, with a stress-ng multi-core score of 93258.02 and a PassMark CPU score of 76019.64. Single-core performance is moderate, as evidenced by a Geekbench single-core score of 1711.0. Memory subsystem performance is highly efficient, showing a read-only small block cached bandwidth of 5005.65 GB/sec and a large block uncached write speed of 134.45 GB/sec. Multi-threaded operations scale effectively, demonstrated by a bzip3 decompression rate of 422.44 GB/sec and strong software compilation capabilities.

Given the 1:2 memory-to-vCPU ratio, this server is designed for compute-heavy workloads that do not require massive memory footprints. The inclusion of 3686 GB of local storage combined with strong database operation benchmarks and high Redis SET throughput makes it highly suitable for high-throughput database hosting, web serving, and parallel build systems. However, the low memory allocation per core represents a tradeoff for memory-intensive in-memory databases or large-scale data analytics.
description: Microsoft Azure D96lds_v5 is a compute-optimized virtual machine powered by an Intel Xeon 8370C processor with 96 dedicated vCPUs and 48 physical cores running at 2.8 GHz. It is configured with 192.0 GB of RAM, providing a 2.0 GB per vCPU ratio, and includes 3686 GB of local storage. Operating on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor, this instance delivers strong multi-core performance, as demonstrated by a PassMark CPU score of 76019.64 and a multi-core Geekbench score of 17699.0. It also exhibits high memory bandwidth and strong database operations throughput. The server is highly suited for compute-intensive tasks, software compilation, static web serving, and database workloads that benefit from fast local storage but do not require high memory-to-CPU ratios.
og_description: Microsoft Azure D96lds_v5 is a compute-optimized VM featuring 96 Intel Xeon vCPUs, 192 GB of RAM, and 3686 GB of local storage, delivering strong multi-core and database performance.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure D96lds_v5 is a compute-optimized VM featuring 96 Intel Xeon vCPUs, 192 GB of RAM, and 3686 GB of local storage for high-throughput workloads.
tagline: A high-core compute-optimized instance featuring dense local storage and strong multi-threaded performance for database and compilation workloads.
bullet_points: 96 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs and 192 GB RAM configuration
3686 GB of bundled local storage for high-speed data access
Strong multi-core processing and high-bandwidth memory performance
Optimized for software compilation, database operations, and web serving
Low memory-to-vCPU ratio tailored for compute-heavy tasks
